     108                 :            : /*
     109                 :            :  * ON COMMIT action list
     110                 :            :  */
     111                 :            : typedef struct OnCommitItem
     112                 :            : {
     113                 :            :         Oid                     relid;                  /* relid of relation */
     114                 :            :         OnCommitAction oncommit;        /* what to do at end of xact */
     115                 :            : 
     116                 :            :         /*
     117                 :            :          * If this entry was created during the current transaction,
     118                 :            :          * creating_subid is the ID of the creating subxact; if created in a prior
     119                 :            :          * transaction, creating_subid is zero.  If deleted during the current
     120                 :            :          * transaction, deleting_subid is the ID of the deleting subxact; if no
     121                 :            :          * deletion request is pending, deleting_subid is zero.
     122                 :            :          */
     123                 :            :         SubTransactionId creating_subid;
     124                 :            :         SubTransactionId deleting_subid;
     125                 :            : } OnCommitItem;
     126                 :            : 
     127                 :            : static List *on_commits = NIL;
     128                 :            : 
     129                 :            : 
     130                 :            : /*
     131                 :            :  * State information for ALTER TABLE
     132                 :            :  *
     133                 :            :  * The pending-work queue for an ALTER TABLE is a List of AlteredTableInfo
     134                 :            :  * structs, one for each table modified by the operation (the named table
     135                 :            :  * plus any child tables that are affected).  We save lists of subcommands
     136                 :            :  * to apply to this table (possibly modified by parse transformation steps);
     137                 :            :  * these lists will be executed in Phase 2.  If a Phase 3 step is needed,
     138                 :            :  * necessary information is stored in the constraints and newvals lists.
     139                 :            :  *
     140                 :            :  * Phase 2 is divided into multiple passes; subcommands are executed in
     141                 :            :  * a pass determined by subcommand type.
     142                 :            :  */
     143                 :            : 
     144                 :            : #define AT_PASS_DROP                    0               /* DROP (all flavors) */
     145                 :            : #define AT_PASS_ALTER_TYPE              1               /* ALTER COLUMN TYPE */
     146                 :            : #define AT_PASS_OLD_INDEX               2               /* re-add existing indexes */
     147                 :            : #define AT_PASS_OLD_CONSTR              3               /* re-add existing constraints */
     148                 :            : #define AT_PASS_COL_ATTRS               4               /* set other column attributes */
     149                 :            : /* We could support a RENAME COLUMN pass here, but not currently used */
     150                 :            : #define AT_PASS_ADD_COL                 5               /* ADD COLUMN */
     151                 :            : #define AT_PASS_ADD_INDEX               6               /* ADD indexes */
     152                 :            : #define AT_PASS_ADD_CONSTR              7               /* ADD constraints, defaults */
     153                 :            : #define AT_PASS_MISC                    8               /* other stuff */
     154                 :            : #define AT_NUM_PASSES                   9
     155                 :            : 
     156                 :            : typedef struct AlteredTableInfo
     157                 :            : {
     158                 :            :         /* Information saved before any work commences: */
     159                 :            :         Oid                     relid;                  /* Relation to work on */
     160                 :            :         char            relkind;                /* Its relkind */
     161                 :            :         TupleDesc       oldDesc;                /* Pre-modification tuple descriptor */
     162                 :            :         /* Information saved by Phase 1 for Phase 2: */
     163                 :            :         List       *subcmds[AT_NUM_PASSES]; /* Lists of AlterTableCmd */
     164                 :            :         /* Information saved by Phases 1/2 for Phase 3: */
     165                 :            :         List       *constraints;        /* List of NewConstraint */
     166                 :            :         List       *newvals;            /* List of NewColumnValue */
     167                 :            :         bool            new_notnull;    /* T if we added new NOT NULL constraints */
     168                 :            :         bool            new_dropoids;   /* T if we dropped the OID column */
     169                 :            :         Oid                     newTableSpace;  /* new tablespace; 0 means no change */
     170                 :            :         Oid                     exchange_relid; /* for EXCHANGE, the exchanged in rel */
     171                 :            :         /* Objects to rebuild after completing ALTER TYPE operations */
     172                 :            :         List       *changedConstraintOids;      /* OIDs of constraints to rebuild */
     173                 :            :         List       *changedConstraintDefs;      /* string definitions of same */
     174                 :            :         List       *changedIndexOids;           /* OIDs of indexes to rebuild */
     175                 :            :         List       *changedIndexDefs;           /* string definitions of same */
     176                 :            : } AlteredTableInfo;
     177                 :            : 
     178                 :            : /*
     179                 :            :  * Struct describing one new column value that needs to be computed during
     180                 :            :  * Phase 3 copy (this could be either a new column with a non-null default, or
     181                 :            :  * a column that we're changing the type of).  Columns without such an entry
     182                 :            :  * are just copied from the old table during ATRewriteTable.  Note that the
     183                 :            :  * expr is an expression over *old* table values.
     184                 :            :  */
     185                 :            : typedef struct NewColumnValue
     186                 :            : {
     187                 :            :         AttrNumber      attnum;                 /* which column */
     188                 :            :         Expr       *expr;                       /* expression to compute */
     189                 :            :         ExprState  *exprstate;          /* execution state */
     190                 :            : } NewColumnValue;
     191                 :            :

     196                 :            :                                                                                 bool is_partition);

     363                 :            : static void change_dropped_col_datatypes(Relation rel);
     364                 :            : 
     365                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     366                 :            :  *              DefineRelation
     367                 :            :  *                              Creates a new relation.
     368                 :            :  *
     369                 :            :  * If successful, returns the OID of the new relation.
     370                 :            :  *
     371                 :            :  * If 'dispatch' is true (and we are running in QD), the statement is
     372                 :            :  * also dispatched to the QE nodes. Otherwise it is the caller's
     373                 :            :  * responsibility to dispatch.
     374                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     375                 :            :  */
     376                 :            : Oid
     377                 :            : DefineRelation(CreateStmt *stmt, char relkind, char relstorage, bool dispatch)
     378                 :            : {
     379                 :            :         char            relname[NAMEDATALEN];
     380                 :            :         Oid                     namespaceId;
     381                 :          0 :         Oid                     relationId = InvalidOid;
     382                 :            :         Oid                     tablespaceId;
     383                 :            :         Relation        rel;
     384                 :            :         TupleDesc       descriptor;
     385                 :            :         List       *old_constraints;
     386                 :            :         bool            localHasOids;
     387                 :            :         List       *colDefaults;
     388                 :            :         Datum           reloptions;
     389                 :            :         ListCell   *listptr;
     390                 :            :         AttrNumber      attnum;
     391                 :            :         bool            isPartitioned;
     392                 :            : 
     393                 :            :         ItemPointerData persistentTid;
     394                 :            :         int64                   persistentSerialNum;
     395                 :            : 
     396         [ #  # ]:          0 :         bool            shouldDispatch = dispatch &&
     397         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                                  Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH &&
     398                 :          0 :                                  IsNormalProcessingMode();
     399                 :            : 
     400                 :            :         /*
     401                 :            :          * Truncate relname to appropriate length (probably a waste of time, as
     402                 :            :          * parser should have done this already).
     403                 :            :          */
     404         [ #  # ]:          0 :         StrNCpy(relname, stmt->relation->relname, NAMEDATALEN);
     405                 :            : 
     406                 :            :         /*
     407                 :            :          * Check consistency of arguments
     408                 :            :          */
     409 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt->oncommit != ONCOMMIT_NOOP && !stmt->relation->istemp)
     410         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     411                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
     412                 :            :                                  errmsg("ON COMMIT can only be used on temporary tables")));
     413                 :            : 
     414                 :            :         /*
     415                 :            :          * Security check: disallow creating temp tables from security-restricted
     416                 :            :          * code.  This is needed because calling code might not expect untrusted
     417                 :            :          * tables to appear in pg_temp at the front of its search path.
     418                 :            :          */
     419 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt->relation->istemp && InSecurityRestrictedOperation())
     420         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     421                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
     422                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot create temporary table within security-restricted operation")));
     423                 :            : 
     424                 :            :         /*
     425                 :            :          * Look up the namespace in which we are supposed to create the relation.
     426                 :            :          * Check we have permission to create there. Skip check if bootstrapping,
     427                 :            :          * since permissions machinery may not be working yet.
     428                 :            :          */
     429                 :          0 :         namespaceId = RangeVarGetCreationNamespace(stmt->relation);
     430                 :            : 
     431         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
     432                 :            :         {
     433                 :            :                 AclResult       aclresult;
     434                 :            : 
     435                 :          0 :                 aclresult = pg_namespace_aclcheck(namespaceId, GetUserId(),
     436                 :            :                                                                                   ACL_CREATE);
     437         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
     438                 :          0 :                         aclcheck_error(aclresult, ACL_KIND_NAMESPACE,
     439                 :          0 :                                                    get_namespace_name(namespaceId));
     440                 :          0 :         }
     441                 :            : 
     442                 :            :         /*
     443                 :            :          * Select tablespace to use.  If not specified, use default tablespace
     444                 :            :          * (which may in turn default to database's default).
     445                 :            :          *
     446                 :            :          * Note: This code duplicates code in indexcmds.c
     447                 :            :          */
     448 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (relkind == RELKIND_SEQUENCE || 
     449         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 relkind == RELKIND_VIEW ||
     450         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 relkind == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE ||
     451         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 (relkind == RELKIND_RELATION && (
     452         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         relstorage == RELSTORAGE_EXTERNAL ||
     453         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         relstorage == RELSTORAGE_FOREIGN  ||
     454                 :          0 :                         relstorage == RELSTORAGE_VIRTUAL)))
     455                 :            :         {
     456                 :            :                 /* 
     457                 :            :                  * MPP-8262: unable to create sequence with default_tablespace
     458                 :            :                  *
     459                 :            :                  * These relkinds have no storage, and thus do not support tablespaces.
     460                 :            :                  * We shouldn't go through the regular default case for these because we
     461                 :            :                  * don't want to pick up the value from the default_tablespace guc.
     462                 :            :                  */
     463 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(!stmt->tablespacename);
     464                 :          0 :                 tablespaceId = InvalidOid;
     465                 :          0 :         }
     466         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (stmt->tablespacename)
     467                 :            :         {
     468                 :            :                 /*
     469                 :            :                  * Tablespace specified on the command line, or was passed down by
     470                 :            :                  * dispatch.
     471                 :            :                  */
     472                 :          0 :                 tablespaceId = get_tablespace_oid(stmt->tablespacename, false);
     473                 :          0 :         }
     474                 :            :         else
     475                 :            :         {
     476                 :            :                 /*
     477                 :            :                  * Get the default tablespace specified via default_tablespace, or fall
     478                 :            :                  * back on the database tablespace.
     479                 :            :                  */
     480                 :          0 :                 tablespaceId = GetDefaultTablespace(stmt->relation->istemp);
     481                 :            : 
     482                 :            :                 /* Need the real tablespace id for dispatch */
     483         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!OidIsValid(tablespaceId)) 
     484                 :          0 :                         tablespaceId = MyDatabaseTableSpace;
     485                 :            : 
     486                 :            :                 /* 
     487                 :            :                  * MPP-8238 : inconsistent tablespaces between segments and master 
     488                 :            :                  */
     489         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (shouldDispatch)
     490                 :          0 :                         stmt->tablespacename = get_tablespace_name(tablespaceId);
     491                 :            :         }
     492                 :            : 
     493                 :            :         /* Check permissions except when using database's default */
     494 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (OidIsValid(tablespaceId) && tablespaceId != MyDatabaseTableSpace)
     495                 :            :         {
     496                 :            :                 AclResult       aclresult;
     497                 :            : 
     498                 :          0 :                 aclresult = pg_tablespace_aclcheck(tablespaceId, GetUserId(),
     499                 :            :                                                                                    ACL_CREATE);
     500         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
     501                 :          0 :                         aclcheck_error(aclresult, ACL_KIND_TABLESPACE,
     502                 :          0 :                                                    get_tablespace_name(tablespaceId));
     503                 :          0 :         }
     504                 :            : 
     505                 :            :         /*
     506                 :            :          * Parse and validate reloptions, if any.
     507                 :            :          */
     508                 :          0 :         reloptions = transformRelOptions((Datum) 0, stmt->options, true, false);
     509                 :            : 
     510                 :            :         /*
     511                 :            :          * Look up inheritance ancestors and generate relation schema, including
     512                 :            :          * inherited attributes. Update the offsets of the distribution attributes
     513                 :            :          * in GpPolicy if necessary. Save result to stmt->tableElts and dispatch to
     514                 :            :          * QEs.
     515                 :            :          */

     517 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH || Gp_role == GP_ROLE_UTILITY)
     518                 :          0 :                 stmt->tableElts = MergeAttributes(stmt->tableElts, stmt->inhRelations,
     519                 :          0 :                                                                                   stmt->relation->istemp, isPartitioned,
     520                 :          0 :                                                                                   &stmt->inhOids, &old_constraints,
     521                 :          0 :                                                                                   &stmt->parentOidCount, stmt->policy);
     522                 :            : 
     523                 :            :         /*
     524                 :            :          * Create a relation descriptor from the relation schema and create the
     525                 :            :          * relation.
     526                 :            :          *
     527                 :            :          * Inherited (pre-cooked) defaults and constraints will not be
     528                 :            :          * included into the new relation in heap_create_with_catalog. Instead, we
     529                 :            :          * add them along with raw defaults and constraints.
     530                 :            :          */
     531                 :          0 :         descriptor = BuildDescForRelation(stmt->tableElts);
     532                 :            : 
     533                 :          0 :         localHasOids = interpretOidsOption(stmt->options);
     534         [ #  # ]:          0 :         descriptor->tdhasoid = (localHasOids || stmt->parentOidCount > 0);
     535                 :            : 
     536                 :            :         /*
     537                 :            :          * old_constraints: pre-cooked constraints from CREATE TABLE ... INHERIT ...
     538                 :            :          * stmt->constraints: might have some pre-cooked constraints passed by parse_utilcmd.c,
     539                 :            :          *              due to LIKE tab INCLUDING CONSTRAINTS
     540                 :            :          */
     541 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if ((Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH || Gp_role == GP_ROLE_UTILITY)
     542         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         && old_constraints)
     543                 :            :         {
     544                 :            :                 /* deal with constraints from MergeAttributes */
     545         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 foreach(listptr, old_constraints)
     546                 :            :                 {
     547                 :          0 :                         Constraint *cdef = (Constraint *) lfirst(listptr);
     548 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (cdef->contype == CONSTR_CHECK &&
     549                 :          0 :                                         add_nonduplicate_cooked_constraint(cdef, stmt->constraints))
     550                 :          0 :                                 stmt->constraints = lappend(stmt->constraints, cdef);
     551                 :          0 :                 }
     552                 :          0 :         }
     553                 :            : 
     554                 :            : 
     555         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (shouldDispatch)
     556                 :            :         {
     557                 :            :                 Relation        pg_class_desc;
     558                 :            :                 Relation        pg_type_desc;
     559                 :            :                 MemoryContext oldContext;
     560                 :            : 
     561                 :            :                 /*
     562                 :            :                  * We use a RowExclusiveLock but hold it till end of transaction so that two
     563                 :            :                  * DDL operations will not deadlock between QEs
     564                 :            :                  */
     565                 :          0 :                 pg_class_desc = heap_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     566                 :          0 :                 pg_type_desc = heap_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     567                 :            : 
     568                 :          0 :                 LockRelationOid(DependRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     569                 :            : 
     570                 :          0 :                 cdb_sync_oid_to_segments();
     571                 :            : 
     572                 :          0 :                 heap_close(pg_class_desc, NoLock);  /* gonna update, so don't unlock */
     573                 :            : 
     574                 :          0 :                 stmt->relKind = relkind;
     575                 :          0 :                 stmt->relStorage = relstorage;
     576                 :            : 
     577         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!OidIsValid(stmt->ownerid))
     578                 :          0 :                         stmt->ownerid = GetUserId();
     579                 :            : 
     580                 :          0 :                 oldContext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     581                 :          0 :                 stmt->relation->schemaname = get_namespace_name(namespaceId);
     582                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldContext);
     583                 :            : 
     584                 :          0 :                 heap_close(pg_type_desc, NoLock);
     585                 :          0 :         }
     586         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE)
     587                 :            :         {
     588 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(stmt->ownerid != InvalidOid);
     589                 :          0 :         }
     590                 :            :         else
     591                 :            :         {
     592         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!OidIsValid(stmt->ownerid))
     593                 :          0 :                         stmt->ownerid = GetUserId();
     594                 :            :         }
     595                 :            : 
     596                 :            :         /* MPP-8405: disallow OIDS on partitioned tables */
     597 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if ((stmt->partitionBy ||
     598         [ #  # ]:          0 :                  stmt->is_part_child) &&
     599         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 descriptor->tdhasoid && 
     600         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 IsNormalProcessingMode() &&
     601                 :          0 :         (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH))
     602         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     603                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
     604                 :            :                                          errmsg(
     605                 :            :                                                          "OIDS=TRUE is not allowed on partitioned tables. "
     606                 :            :                                                          "Use OIDS=FALSE"
     607                 :            :                                                          )));
     608                 :            : 
     609                 :          0 :         bool valid_opts = (relstorage == RELSTORAGE_EXTERNAL);
     610                 :            : 
     611                 :          0 :         relationId = heap_create_with_catalog(relname,
     612                 :          0 :                                                                                   namespaceId,
     613                 :          0 :                                                                                   tablespaceId,
     614                 :            :                                                                                   InvalidOid,
     615                 :          0 :                                                                                   stmt->ownerid,
     616                 :          0 :                                                                                   descriptor,
     617                 :            :                                                                                   /* relam */ InvalidOid,
     618                 :          0 :                                                                                   relkind,
     619                 :          0 :                                                                                   relstorage,
     620                 :          0 :                                                                                   tablespaceId==GLOBALTABLESPACE_OID,
     621                 :          0 :                                                                                   localHasOids,
     622                 :            :                                                                                   /* bufferPoolBulkLoad */ false,
     623                 :          0 :                                                                                   stmt->parentOidCount,
     624                 :          0 :                                                                                   stmt->oncommit,
     625                 :          0 :                                           stmt->policy,  /*CDB*/
     626                 :          0 :                                           reloptions,
     627                 :          0 :                                                                                   allowSystemTableModsDDL,
     628                 :          0 :                                                                                   valid_opts,
     629                 :            :                                                                                   &persistentTid,
     630                 :            :                                                                                   &persistentSerialNum);
     631                 :            : 
     632                 :          0 :         StoreCatalogInheritance(relationId, stmt->inhOids);
     633                 :            : 
     634                 :            :         /*
     635                 :            :          * We must bump the command counter to make the newly-created relation
     636                 :            :          * tuple visible for opening.
     637                 :            :          */
     638                 :          0 :         CommandCounterIncrement();
     639                 :            : 
     640                 :            :         /*
     641                 :            :          * Open the new relation and acquire exclusive lock on it.  This isn't
     642                 :            :          * really necessary for locking out other backends (since they can't see
     643                 :            :          * the new rel anyway until we commit), but it keeps the lock manager from
     644                 :            :          * complaining about deadlock risks.
     645                 :            :          *
     646                 :            :          * GPDB: Don't lock it if we're creating a partition, however. Creating a
     647                 :            :          * heavily-partitioned table would otherwise acquire a lot of locks.
     648                 :            :          */
     649         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt->is_part_child)
     650                 :          0 :                 rel = relation_open(relationId, NoLock);
     651                 :            :         else
     652                 :          0 :                 rel = relation_open(relationId, AccessExclusiveLock);
     653                 :            : 
     654                 :            :         /*
     655                 :            :          * For some reason, even though we have bumped the command counter above, 
     656                 :            :          * occasionally we are not able to see the persistent info just stored in gp_relation_node
     657                 :            :          * at the XLOG level.  So, we save the values here for debugging purposes.
     658                 :            :          */
     659                 :          0 :         rel->rd_haveCreateDebugInfo = true;
     660                 :          0 :         rel->rd_createDebugIsZeroTid = PersistentStore_IsZeroTid(&persistentTid);
     661                 :          0 :         rel->rd_createDebugPersistentTid = persistentTid;
     662                 :          0 :         rel->rd_createDebugPersistentSerialNum = persistentSerialNum;
     663                 :            : 
     664                 :            :         /*
     665                 :            :          * Now add any newly specified column default values and CHECK constraints
     666                 :            :          * to the new relation.  These are passed to us in the form of raw or cooked
     667                 :            :          * parsetrees; we need to transform them if they are raw to executable expression
     668                 :            :          * trees before they can be added. The most convenient way to do that is to
     669                 :            :          * apply the parser's transformExpr routine, but transformExpr doesn't
     670                 :            :          * work unless we have a pre-existing relation. So, the transformation has
     671                 :            :          * to be postponed to this final step of CREATE TABLE.
     672                 :            :          *
     673                 :            :          * First, scan schema to find new column defaults.
     674                 :            :          */
     675                 :          0 :         colDefaults = NIL;
     676                 :          0 :         attnum = 0;
     677                 :            : 
     678         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(listptr, stmt->tableElts)
     679                 :            :         {
     680                 :          0 :                 ColumnDef  *colDef = lfirst(listptr);
     681                 :            : 
     682                 :          0 :                 colDef->attnum = ++attnum;
     683 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (colDef->raw_default != NULL || colDef->cooked_default != NULL)
     684                 :          0 :                         colDefaults = lappend(colDefaults, colDef);
     685                 :          0 :         }
     686                 :            : 
     687                 :            :         /*
     688                 :            :          * Parse and add the defaults/constraints, if any.
     689                 :            :          */
     690 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (colDefaults || stmt->constraints)
     691                 :          0 :                 AddRelationConstraints(rel, colDefaults, stmt->constraints);
     692                 :            : 
     693         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt->attr_encodings)
     694                 :          0 :                 AddRelationAttributeEncodings(rel, stmt->attr_encodings);
     695                 :            : 
     696                 :            :         /* It is now safe to dispatch */
     697         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (shouldDispatch)
     698                 :            :         {
     699                 :            :                 /*
     700                 :            :                  * Dispatch the statement tree to all primary and mirror segdbs.
     701                 :            :                  * Doesn't wait for the QEs to finish execution.
     702                 :            :                  *
     703                 :            :                  * The OIDs are carried out-of-band.
     704                 :            :                  */
     705                 :          0 :                 CdbDispatchUtilityStatement((Node *) stmt,
     706                 :            :                                                                         DF_CANCEL_ON_ERROR |
     707                 :            :                                                                         DF_NEED_TWO_PHASE |
     708                 :            :                                                                         DF_WITH_SNAPSHOT,
     709                 :          0 :                                                                         GetAssignedOidsForDispatch(),
     710                 :            :                                                                         NULL);
     711                 :          0 :         }
     712                 :            : 
     713                 :            :         /*
     714                 :            :          * Clean up.  We keep lock on new relation (although it shouldn't be
     715                 :            :          * visible to anyone else anyway, until commit).
     716                 :            :          */
     717                 :          0 :         relation_close(rel, NoLock);
     718                 :            : 
     719                 :          0 :         return relationId;
     720                 :            : }
     721                 :            : 
     722                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     723                 :            : *               DefinePartitionedRelation
     724                 :            : *                               Create the rewrite rule for a partitioned table
     725                 :            : *
     726                 :            : * parse_partition.c/transformPartitionBy does the bulk of the work for
     727                 :            : * partitioned tables, converting a single CREATE TABLE into a series
     728                 :            : * of statements to create the child tables for each partition.  Each
     729                 :            : * child table has a check constraint and a rewrite rule to ensure that
     730                 :            : * INSERTs to the parent end up in the correct child table (partition).
     731                 :            : * However, we cannot add a RuleStmt for a non-existent table to the
     732                 :            : * a(fter)list for the Create statement (believe me, I tried, really
     733                 :            : * hard).  Thus, we create the "parsed" RuleStmt in analyze, and
     734                 :            : * finally parse_analyze it here *after* the relation is created, the
     735                 :            : * use process_utility to dispatch.
     736                 :            : * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     737                 :            : */
     738                 :            : void
     739                 :            : DefinePartitionedRelation(CreateStmt *stmt, Oid relOid)
     740                 :            : {
     741                 :            : 
     742         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt->postCreate)
     743                 :            :         {
     744                 :            :                 Query      *pUtl;
     745                 :          0 :                 DestReceiver *dest = None_Receiver;
     746                 :          0 :                 List *pL1 = (List *)stmt->postCreate;
     747                 :            : 
     748                 :          0 :                 pUtl = parse_analyze(linitial(pL1), NULL, NULL, 0);
     749                 :            : 
     750 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(((Query *)pUtl)->commandType == CMD_UTILITY);
     751                 :            : 
     752                 :          0 :                 ProcessUtility((Node *)(((Query *)pUtl)->utilityStmt),
     753                 :          0 :                                            synthetic_sql,
     754                 :            :                                            NULL,
     755                 :            :                                            false, /* not top level */
     756                 :          0 :                                            dest,
     757                 :            :                                            NULL);
     758                 :          0 :         }
     759                 :          0 : }
     760                 :            : 
     761                 :            : /*
     762                 :            :  * Run deferred statements generated by internal operations around
     763                 :            :  * partition addition/split.
     764                 :            :  */
     765                 :            : void
     766                 :            : EvaluateDeferredStatements(List *deferredStmts)
     767                 :            : {
     768                 :            :         ListCell           *lc;
     769                 :            : 
     770                 :            :         /***
     771                 :            :          *** XXX: Fix MPP-13750, however this fails to address the similar bug
     772                 :            :          ***      with ordinary inheritance and partial indexes.  When that bug,
     773                 :            :          ***      is fixed, this section should become unnecessary!
     774                 :            :          ***/
     775                 :            :         
     776         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach( lc, deferredStmts )
     777                 :            :         {
     778                 :            :                 Query      *uquery;
     779                 :          0 :                 DestReceiver *dest = None_Receiver;
     780                 :            : 
     781                 :          0 :                 Node *dstmt = lfirst(lc);
     782                 :            : 
     783                 :          0 :                 uquery = parse_analyze(dstmt, NULL, NULL, 0);
     784         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 Insist(uquery->commandType == CMD_UTILITY);
     785                 :            : 
     786         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG1,
     787                 :            :                                 (errmsg("processing deferred utility statement")));
     788                 :            : 
     789                 :          0 :                 ProcessUtility((Node*)uquery->utilityStmt,
     790                 :          0 :                                            synthetic_sql,
     791                 :            :                                            NULL,
     792                 :            :                                            false,
     793                 :          0 :                                            dest,
     794                 :            :                                            NULL);
     795                 :          0 :         }
     796                 :          0 : }
     797                 :            : 
     798                 :            : /* Don't track internal namespaces for toast, bitmap, aoseg */
     799                 :            : #define METATRACK_VALIDNAMESPACE(namespaceId) \
     800                 :            :         (namespaceId != PG_TOAST_NAMESPACE &&   \
     801                 :            :          namespaceId != PG_BITMAPINDEX_NAMESPACE && \
     802                 :            :          namespaceId != PG_AOSEGMENT_NAMESPACE )
     803                 :            : 
     804                 :            : /* check for valid namespace and valid relkind */
     805                 :            : static 
     806                 :            : bool
     807                 :            : MetaTrackValidKindNsp(Form_pg_class rd_rel)
     808                 :            : {
     809                 :          0 :         Oid nsp = rd_rel->relnamespace;
     810                 :            : 
     811         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (PG_CATALOG_NAMESPACE == nsp)
     812                 :            :         {
     813                 :            :                 /* MPP-7773: don't track objects in system namespace
     814                 :            :                  * if modifying system tables (eg during upgrade)  
     815                 :            :                  */
     816         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (allowSystemTableModsDDL)
     817                 :          0 :                         return (false);
     818                 :          0 :         }
     819                 :            : 
     820                 :            :         /* MPP-7599: watch out for toast indexes */
     821 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         return (METATRACK_VALIDNAMESPACE(nsp)
     822 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         && MetaTrackValidRelkind(rd_rel->relkind)
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
     823                 :            :                         /* MPP-7572: not valid if in any temporary namespace */
     824         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         && (!(isAnyTempNamespace(nsp))));
     825                 :          0 : }
     826                 :            : 
     827                 :            : /*
     828                 :            :  * RemoveRelation
     829                 :            :  *              Deletes a relation.
     830                 :            :  */
     831                 :            : bool
     832                 :            : RemoveRelation(const RangeVar *relation, DropBehavior behavior,
     833                 :            :                            DropStmt *stmt, char relkind)
     834                 :            : {
     835                 :            :         Oid                     relOid;
     836                 :            :         ObjectAddress object;
     837                 :            : 
     838                 :          0 :         AcceptInvalidationMessages();
     839                 :            : 
     840         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)
     841                 :            :         {
     842                 :          0 :                 LockRelationOid(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     843                 :          0 :                 LockRelationOid(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     844                 :          0 :                 LockRelationOid(DependRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     845                 :          0 :         }
     846                 :            : 
     847                 :            :         /*
     848                 :            :          * Perform name lookup again if we had to wait to acquire lock on
     849                 :            :          * OID of the relation.  The relation's name could have been
     850                 :            :          * altered while we were waiting.
     851                 :            :          */
     852                 :          0 :         relOid = RangeVarGetRelidExtended(
     853         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         relation, AccessExclusiveLock, stmt?stmt->missing_ok:false,
     854                 :            :                         false /* nowait */, NULL /* callback */,
     855                 :            :                         NULL /* callback_arg */);
     856                 :            : 
     857         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!OidIsValid(relOid))
     858                 :            :         {
     859                 :            :                 /*
     860                 :            :                  * Missed to find the object to be dropped.
     861                 :            :                  * Drop with "if exists" just notify the same, unlock as not performing
     862                 :            :                  * any operation and return back to convey didn't drop the relation.
     863                 :            :                  * Drop without "if exists" won't even come here, as would error
     864                 :            :                  * inside RangeVarGetRelidExtended.
     865                 :            :                  */
     866         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 DropErrorMsgNonExistent(relation, relkind, stmt?stmt->missing_ok:false);
     867         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)
     868                 :            :                 {
     869                 :          0 :                         UnlockRelationOid(DependRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     870                 :          0 :                         UnlockRelationOid(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     871                 :          0 :                         UnlockRelationOid(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
     872                 :          0 :                 }
     873                 :          0 :                 return false;
     874                 :            :         }
     875                 :            : 
     876                 :            :         /* MPP-3260: disallow direct DROP TABLE of a partition */
     877 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (stmt && rel_is_child_partition(relOid) && !stmt->bAllowPartn)
                 [ #  # ]
     878                 :            :         {
     879                 :          0 :                 Oid              master = rel_partition_get_master(relOid);
     880                 :          0 :                 char    *pretty = rel_get_part_path_pretty(relOid,
     881                 :            :                                                                                                    " ALTER PARTITION ",
     882                 :            :                                                                                                    " DROP PARTITION ");
     883                 :            : 
     884 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     885                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DEPENDENT_OBJECTS_STILL_EXIST),
     886                 :            :                                 errmsg("cannot drop partition \"%s\" directly",
     887                 :            :                                            get_rel_name(relOid)),
     888                 :            :                                 errhint("Table \"%s\" is a child partition of table "

     890                 :            :                                                 get_rel_name(relOid), get_rel_name(master),

     892                 :            : 
     893                 :          0 :         }
     894                 :            : 
     895                 :          0 :         object.classId = RelationRelationId;
     896                 :          0 :         object.objectId = relOid;
     897                 :          0 :         object.objectSubId = 0;
     898                 :            : 
     899                 :            :         /* if we got here then we should proceed. */
     900                 :          0 :         performDeletion(&object, behavior);
     901                 :          0 :         return true;
     902                 :          0 : }

     904                 :            : /*
     905                 :            :  * RelationToRemoveIsTemp

     907                 :            :  */
     908                 :            : bool
     909                 :            : RelationToRemoveIsTemp(const RangeVar *relation, DropBehavior behavior)
     910                 :            : {
     911                 :            :         Oid                     relOid;
     912                 :            :         Oid                     recheckoid;

     915                 :            :         Form_pg_class relForm;
     916                 :            :         char       *nspname;
     917                 :            :         char       *relname;
