    1775         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    1776                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_TOO_MANY_COLUMNS),
    1777                 :            :                                          errmsg("tables can have at most %d columns",
    1778                 :            :                                                         MaxHeapAttributeNumber)));
    1779                 :          0 :         }
    1780                 :            : 
    1781                 :            :         /*
    1782                 :            :          * If we found any conflicting parent default values, check to make sure
    1783                 :            :          * they were overridden by the child.
    1784                 :            :          */
    1785         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (have_bogus_defaults)
    1786                 :            :         {
    1787         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 foreach(entry, schema)
    1788                 :            :                 {
    1789                 :          0 :                         ColumnDef  *def = lfirst(entry);
    1790                 :            : 
    1791         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (def->cooked_default == bogus_marker)
    1792         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
    1793                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_DEFINITION),
    1794                 :            :                                   errmsg("column \"%s\" inherits conflicting default values",
    1795                 :            :                                                  def->colname),
    1796                 :            :                                                  errhint("To resolve the conflict, specify a default explicitly.")));
    1797                 :          0 :                 }
    1798                 :          0 :         }
    1799                 :            : 
    1800                 :          0 :         *supOids = parentOids;
    1801                 :          0 :         *supconstr = constraints;
    1802                 :          0 :         *supOidCount = parentsWithOids;
    1803                 :          0 :         return schema;
    1804                 :            : }
    1805                 :            : 
    1806                 :            : 
    1807                 :            : /*
    1808                 :            :  * In multiple-inheritance situations, it's possible to inherit
    1809                 :            :  * the same grandparent constraint through multiple parents.
    1810                 :            :  * Hence, we want to discard inherited constraints that match as to
    1811                 :            :  * both name and expression.  Otherwise, gripe if there are conflicting
    1812                 :            :  * names. Nonconflicting constraints are added back to the CreateStmt->constraints
    1813                 :            :  */
    1814                 :            : static bool
    1815                 :            : add_nonduplicate_cooked_constraint(Constraint *cdef, List *stmtConstraints)
    1816                 :            : {
    1817                 :            :         /* Should only see precooked constraints here */
    1818 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(cdef->contype == CONSTR_CHECK);
    1819 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(cdef->name != NULL);
    1820 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(cdef->raw_expr == NULL && cdef->cooked_expr != NULL);
                 [ #  # ]
    1821                 :            : 
    1822                 :            :         ListCell        *listptr;
    1823                 :            : 
    1824         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(listptr, stmtConstraints)
    1825                 :            :         {
    1826                 :          0 :                 Constraint *stmtCdef = (Constraint *) lfirst(listptr);
    1827                 :            : 
    1828                 :            :                 /* Constraint in CreateStmt may be raw and maynot be a check constraint */
    1829 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (stmtCdef->cooked_expr == NULL || stmtCdef->contype != CONSTR_CHECK)
    1830                 :          0 :                         continue;
    1831         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strcmp(stmtCdef->name, cdef->name) != 0)
    1832                 :          0 :                         continue;
    1833         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strcmp(stmtCdef->cooked_expr, cdef->cooked_expr) == 0)
    1834                 :          0 :                         return false;
    1835                 :          0 :         }
    1836                 :          0 :         return true;
    1837                 :          0 : }
    1838                 :            : 
    1839                 :            : 
    1840                 :            : /*
    1841                 :            :  * StoreCatalogInheritance
    1842                 :            :  *              Updates the system catalogs with proper inheritance information.
    1843                 :            :  *
    1844                 :            :  * supers is a list of the OIDs of the new relation's direct ancestors.
    1845                 :            :  */
    1846                 :            : static void
    1847                 :            : StoreCatalogInheritance(Oid relationId, List *supers)
    1848                 :            : {
    1849                 :            :         Relation        relation;
    1850                 :            :         int16           seqNumber;
    1851                 :            :         ListCell   *entry;
    1852                 :            : 
    1853                 :            :         /*
    1854                 :            :          * sanity checks
    1855                 :            :          */
    1856 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         AssertArg(OidIsValid(relationId));
    1857                 :            : 
    1858         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (supers == NIL)
    1859                 :          0 :                 return;
    1860                 :            : 
    1861                 :            :         /*
    1862                 :            :          * Store INHERITS information in pg_inherits using direct ancestors only.
    1863                 :            :          * Also enter dependencies on the direct ancestors, and make sure they are
    1864                 :            :          * marked with relhassubclass = true.
    1865                 :            :          *
    1866                 :            :          * (Once upon a time, both direct and indirect ancestors were found here
    1867                 :            :          * and then entered into pg_ipl.  Since that catalog doesn't exist
    1868                 :            :          * anymore, there's no need to look for indirect ancestors.)
    1869                 :            :          */
    1870                 :          0 :         relation = heap_open(InheritsRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
    1871                 :            : 
    1872                 :          0 :         seqNumber = 1;
    1873         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(entry, supers)
    1874                 :            :         {
    1875                 :          0 :                 Oid                     parentOid = lfirst_oid(entry);
    1876                 :            : 
    1877                 :          0 :                 StoreCatalogInheritance1(relationId, parentOid, seqNumber, relation,
    1878                 :            :                                                                  false);
    1879                 :          0 :                 seqNumber++;
    1880                 :          0 :         }
    1881                 :            : 
    1882                 :          0 :         heap_close(relation, RowExclusiveLock);
    1883                 :          0 : }
    1884                 :            : 
    1885                 :            : /*
    1886                 :            :  * Make catalog entries showing relationId as being an inheritance child
    1887                 :            :  * of parentOid.  inhRelation is the already-opened pg_inherits catalog.
    1888                 :            :  */
    1889                 :            : static void
    1890                 :            : StoreCatalogInheritance1(Oid relationId, Oid parentOid,
    1891                 :            :                                                  int16 seqNumber, Relation inhRelation,
    1892                 :            :                                                  bool is_partition)
    1893                 :            : {
    1894                 :          0 :         TupleDesc       desc = RelationGetDescr(inhRelation);
    1895                 :            :         Datum           datum[Natts_pg_inherits];
    1896                 :            :         bool            nullarr[Natts_pg_inherits];
    1897                 :            :         ObjectAddress childobject,
    1898                 :            :                                 parentobject;
    1899                 :            :         HeapTuple       tuple;
    1900                 :            : 
    1901                 :            :         /*
    1902                 :            :          * Make the pg_inherits entry
    1903                 :            :          */
    1904                 :          0 :         datum[0] = ObjectIdGetDatum(relationId);        /* inhrelid */
    1905                 :          0 :         datum[1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(parentOid);         /* inhparent */
    1906                 :          0 :         datum[2] = Int16GetDatum(seqNumber);            /* inhseqno */
    1907                 :            : 
    1908                 :          0 :         nullarr[0] = false;
    1909                 :          0 :         nullarr[1] = false;
    1910                 :          0 :         nullarr[2] = false;
    1911                 :            : 
    1912                 :          0 :         tuple = heap_form_tuple(desc, datum, nullarr);
    1913                 :            : 
    1914                 :          0 :         simple_heap_insert(inhRelation, tuple);
    1915                 :            : 
    1916                 :          0 :         CatalogUpdateIndexes(inhRelation, tuple);
    1917                 :            : 
    1918                 :          0 :         heap_freetuple(tuple);
    1919                 :            : 
    1920                 :            :         /*
    1921                 :            :          * Store a dependency too
    1922                 :            :          */
    1923                 :          0 :         parentobject.classId = RelationRelationId;
    1924                 :          0 :         parentobject.objectId = parentOid;
    1925                 :          0 :         parentobject.objectSubId = 0;
    1926                 :          0 :         childobject.classId = RelationRelationId;
    1927                 :          0 :         childobject.objectId = relationId;
    1928                 :          0 :         childobject.objectSubId = 0;
    1929                 :            : 
    1930                 :          0 :         recordDependencyOn(&childobject, &parentobject,
    1931                 :          0 :                                            is_partition ? DEPENDENCY_AUTO : DEPENDENCY_NORMAL);
    1932                 :            : 
    1933                 :            :         /*
    1934                 :            :          * Mark the parent as having subclasses.
    1935                 :            :          */
    1936                 :          0 :         setRelhassubclassInRelation(parentOid, true);
    1937                 :          0 : }
    1938                 :            : 
    1939                 :            : /*
    1940                 :            :  * Look for an existing schema entry with the given name.
    1941                 :            :  *
    1942                 :            :  * Returns the index (starting with 1) if attribute already exists in schema,
    1943                 :            :  * 0 if it doesn't.
    1944                 :            :  */
    1945                 :            : static int
    1946                 :            : findAttrByName(const char *attributeName, List *schema)
    1947                 :            : {
    1948                 :            :         ListCell   *s;
    1949                 :          0 :         int                     i = 1;
    1950                 :            : 
    1951         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(s, schema)
    1952                 :            :         {
    1953                 :          0 :                 ColumnDef  *def = lfirst(s);
    1954                 :            : 
    1955         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strcmp(attributeName, def->colname) == 0)
    1956                 :          0 :                         return i;
    1957                 :            : 
    1958                 :          0 :                 i++;
    1959                 :          0 :         }
    1960                 :          0 :         return 0;
    1961                 :          0 : }
    1962                 :            : 
    1963                 :            : /*
    1964                 :            :  * Update a relation's pg_class.relhassubclass entry to the given value
    1965                 :            :  */
    1966                 :            : static void
    1967                 :            : setRelhassubclassInRelation(Oid relationId, bool relhassubclass)
    1968                 :            : {
    1969                 :            :         Relation        relationRelation;
    1970                 :            :         HeapTuple       tuple;
    1971                 :            :         Form_pg_class classtuple;
    1972                 :            : 
    1973                 :            :         /*
    1974                 :            :          * Fetch a modifiable copy of the tuple, modify it, update pg_class.
    1975                 :            :          *
    1976                 :            :          * If the tuple already has the right relhassubclass setting, we don't
    1977                 :            :          * need to update it, but we still need to issue an SI inval message.
    1978                 :            :          */
    1979                 :          0 :         relationRelation = heap_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
    1980                 :          0 :         tuple = SearchSysCacheCopy(RELOID,
    1981                 :          0 :                                                            ObjectIdGetDatum(relationId),
    1982                 :            :                                                            0, 0, 0);
    1983         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    1984                 :          0 :                 elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u", relationId);
    1985                 :          0 :         classtuple = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
    1986                 :            : 
    1987         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (classtuple->relhassubclass != relhassubclass)
    1988                 :            :         {
    1989                 :          0 :                 classtuple->relhassubclass = relhassubclass;
    1990                 :          0 :                 simple_heap_update(relationRelation, &tuple->t_self, tuple);
    1991                 :            : 
    1992                 :            :                 /* keep the catalog indexes up to date */
    1993                 :          0 :                 CatalogUpdateIndexes(relationRelation, tuple);
    1994                 :          0 :         }
    1995                 :            :         else
    1996                 :            :         {
    1997                 :            :                 /* no need to change tuple, but force relcache rebuild anyway */
    1998                 :          0 :                 CacheInvalidateRelcacheByTuple(tuple);
    1999                 :            :         }
    2000                 :            : 
    2001                 :          0 :         heap_freetuple(tuple);
    2002                 :          0 :         heap_close(relationRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
    2003                 :          0 : }
    2004                 :            : 
    2005                 :            : 
    2006                 :            : /*
    2007                 :            :  *              renameatt               - changes the name of a attribute in a relation
    2008                 :            :  *
    2009                 :            :  *              Attname attribute is changed in attribute catalog.
    2010                 :            :  *              No record of the previous attname is kept (correct?).
    2011                 :            :  *
    2012                 :            :  *              get proper relrelation from relation catalog (if not arg)
    2013                 :            :  *              scan attribute catalog
    2014                 :            :  *                              for name conflict (within rel)
    2015                 :            :  *                              for original attribute (if not arg)
    2016                 :            :  *              modify attname in attribute tuple
    2017                 :            :  *              insert modified attribute in attribute catalog
    2018                 :            :  *              delete original attribute from attribute catalog
    2019                 :            :  */
    2020                 :            : void
    2021                 :            : renameatt(Oid myrelid,
    2022                 :            :                   const char *oldattname,
    2023                 :            :                   const char *newattname,
    2024                 :            :                   bool recurse,
    2025                 :            :                   bool recursing)
    2026                 :            : {
    2027                 :            :         Relation        targetrelation;
    2028                 :            :         Relation        attrelation;
    2029                 :            :         HeapTuple       atttup;
    2030                 :            :         Form_pg_attribute attform;
    2031                 :            :         int                     attnum;
    2032                 :            :         List       *indexoidlist;
    2033                 :            :         ListCell   *indexoidscan;
    2034                 :            : 
    2035                 :            :         /*
    2036                 :            :          * Grab an exclusive lock on the target table, which we will NOT release
    2037                 :            :          * until end of transaction.
    2038                 :            :          */
    2039                 :          0 :         targetrelation = relation_open(myrelid, AccessExclusiveLock);
    2040                 :            : 
    2041                 :            :         /*
    2042                 :            :          * permissions checking.  this would normally be done in utility.c, but
    2043                 :            :          * this particular routine is recursive.
    2044                 :            :          *
    2045                 :            :          * normally, only the owner of a class can change its schema.
    2046                 :            :          */
    2047         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!pg_class_ownercheck(myrelid, GetUserId()))
    2048                 :          0 :                 aclcheck_error(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, ACL_KIND_CLASS,
    2049                 :          0 :                                            RelationGetRelationName(targetrelation));
    2050 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!allowSystemTableModsDDL && IsSystemRelation(targetrelation))
    2051         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2052                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
    2053                 :            :                                  errmsg("permission denied: \"%s\" is a system catalog",
    2054                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(targetrelation))));
    2055                 :            : 
    2056                 :            :         /*
    2057                 :            :          * if the 'recurse' flag is set then we are supposed to rename this
    2058                 :            :          * attribute in all classes that inherit from 'relname' (as well as in
    2059                 :            :          * 'relname').
    2060                 :            :          *
    2061                 :            :          * any permissions or problems with duplicate attributes will cause the
    2062                 :            :          * whole transaction to abort, which is what we want -- all or nothing.
    2063                 :            :          */
    2064         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (recurse)
    2065                 :            :         {
    2066                 :            :                 ListCell   *child;
    2067                 :            :                 List       *children;
    2068                 :            : 
    2069                 :            :                 /* this routine is actually in the planner */
    2070                 :          0 :                 children = find_all_inheritors(myrelid);
    2071                 :            : 
    2072                 :            :                 /*
    2073                 :            :                  * find_all_inheritors does the recursive search of the inheritance
    2074                 :            :                  * hierarchy, so all we have to do is process all of the relids in the
    2075                 :            :                  * list that it returns.
    2076                 :            :                  */
    2077         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 foreach(child, children)
    2078                 :            :                 {
    2079                 :          0 :                         Oid                     childrelid = lfirst_oid(child);
    2080                 :            : 
    2081         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (childrelid == myrelid)
    2082                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    2083                 :            :                         /* note we need not recurse again */
    2084                 :          0 :                         renameatt(childrelid, oldattname, newattname, false, true);
    2085                 :          0 :                 }
    2086                 :          0 :         }
    2087                 :            :         else
    2088                 :            :         {
    2089                 :            :                 /*
    2090                 :            :                  * If we are told not to recurse, there had better not be any child
    2091                 :            :                  * tables; else the rename would put them out of step.
    2092                 :            :                  */
    2093 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!recursing &&
    2094                 :          0 :                         find_inheritance_children(myrelid) != NIL)
    2095         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    2096                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
    2097                 :            :                                          errmsg("inherited column \"%s\" must be renamed in child tables too",
    2098                 :            :                                                         oldattname)));
    2099                 :            :         }
    2100                 :            : 
    2101                 :          0 :         attrelation = heap_open(AttributeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
    2102                 :            : 
    2103                 :          0 :         atttup = SearchSysCacheCopyAttName(myrelid, oldattname);
    2104         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(atttup))
    2105         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2106                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
    2107                 :            :                                  errmsg("column \"%s\" does not exist",
    2108                 :            :                                                 oldattname)));
    2109                 :          0 :         attform = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(atttup);
    2110                 :            : 
    2111                 :          0 :         attnum = attform->attnum;
    2112         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (attnum <= 0)
    2113         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2114                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    2115                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot rename system column \"%s\"",
    2116                 :            :                                                 oldattname)));
    2117                 :            : 
    2118                 :            :         /*
    2119                 :            :          * if the attribute is inherited, forbid the renaming, unless we are
    2120                 :            :          * already inside a recursive rename.
    2121                 :            :          */
    2122 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (attform->attinhcount > 0 && !recursing)
    2123         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2124                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
    2125                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot rename inherited column \"%s\"",
    2126                 :            :                                                 oldattname)));
    2127                 :            : 
    2128                 :            :         /* should not already exist */
    2129                 :            :         /* this test is deliberately not attisdropped-aware */
    2130         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (SearchSysCacheExists(ATTNAME,
    2131                 :          0 :                                                          ObjectIdGetDatum(myrelid),
    2132                 :          0 :                                                          PointerGetDatum(newattname),
    2133                 :            :                                                          0, 0))
    2134         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2135                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_COLUMN),
    2136                 :            :                                  errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" already exists",
    2137                 :            :                                           newattname, RelationGetRelationName(targetrelation))));
    2138                 :            : 
    2139                 :          0 :         namestrcpy(&(attform->attname), newattname);
    2140                 :            : 
    2141                 :          0 :         simple_heap_update(attrelation, &atttup->t_self, atttup);
    2142                 :            : 
    2143                 :            :         /* keep system catalog indexes current */
    2144                 :          0 :         CatalogUpdateIndexes(attrelation, atttup);
    2145                 :            : 
    2146                 :          0 :         heap_freetuple(atttup);
    2147                 :            : 
    2148                 :            :         /*
    2149                 :            :          * Update column names of indexes that refer to the column being renamed.
    2150                 :            :          */
    2151                 :          0 :         indexoidlist = RelationGetIndexList(targetrelation);
    2152                 :            : 
    2153         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(indexoidscan, indexoidlist)
    2154                 :            :         {
    2155                 :          0 :                 Oid                     indexoid = lfirst_oid(indexoidscan);
    2156                 :            :                 HeapTuple       indextup;
    2157                 :            :                 Form_pg_index indexform;
    2158                 :            :                 int                     i;
    2159                 :            : 
    2160                 :            :                 /*
    2161                 :            :                  * Scan through index columns to see if there's any simple index
    2162                 :            :                  * entries for this attribute.  We ignore expressional entries.
    2163                 :            :                  */
    2164                 :          0 :                 indextup = SearchSysCache(INDEXRELID,
    2165                 :          0 :                                                                   ObjectIdGetDatum(indexoid),
    2166                 :            :                                                                   0, 0, 0);
    2167         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(indextup))
    2168                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for index %u", indexoid);
    2169                 :          0 :                 indexform = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(indextup);
    2170                 :            : 
    2171         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 for (i = 0; i < indexform->indnatts; i++)
    2172                 :            :                 {
    2173         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (attnum != indexform->indkey.values[i])
    2174                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    2175                 :            : 
    2176                 :            :                         /*
    2177                 :            :                          * Found one, rename it.
    2178                 :            :                          */

    2455                 :            : static bool
    2456                 :            : TypeTupleExists(Oid typeId)
    2457                 :            : {
    2458                 :          0 :         return SearchSysCacheExists(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(typeId), 0, 0, 0);

    2460                 :            : 
    2461                 :            : /*
    2462                 :            :  * Disallow ALTER TABLE (and similar commands) when the current backend has
    2463                 :            :  * any open reference to the target table besides the one just acquired by
    2464                 :            :  * the calling command; this implies there's an open cursor or active plan.
    2465                 :            :  * We need this check because our AccessExclusiveLock doesn't protect us
    2466                 :            :  * against stomping on our own foot, only other people's feet!
    2467                 :            :  *
    2468                 :            :  * For ALTER TABLE, the only case known to cause serious trouble is ALTER
    2469                 :            :  * COLUMN TYPE, and some changes are obviously pretty benign, so this could
    2470                 :            :  * possibly be relaxed to only error out for certain types of alterations.
    2471                 :            :  * But the use-case for allowing any of these things is not obvious, so we
    2472                 :            :  * won't work hard at it for now.
    2473                 :            :  *
    2474                 :            :  * We also reject these commands if there are any pending AFTER trigger events
    2475                 :            :  * for the rel.  This is certainly necessary for the rewriting variants of
    2476                 :            :  * ALTER TABLE, because they don't preserve tuple TIDs and so the pending
    2477                 :            :  * events would try to fetch the wrong tuples.  It might be overly cautious
    2478                 :            :  * in other cases, but again it seems better to err on the side of paranoia.
    2479                 :            :  *
    2480                 :            :  * REINDEX calls this with "rel" referencing the index to be rebuilt; here
    2481                 :            :  * we are worried about active indexscans on the index.  The trigger-event
    2482                 :            :  * check can be skipped, since we are doing no damage to the parent table.
    2483                 :            :  *
    2484                 :            :  * The statement name (eg, "ALTER TABLE") is passed for use in error messages.
    2485                 :            :  */
    2486                 :            : void
    2487                 :            : CheckTableNotInUse(Relation rel, const char *stmt)
    2488                 :            : {
    2489                 :            :         int                     expected_refcnt;
    2490                 :            : 
    2491                 :          0 :         expected_refcnt = rel->rd_isnailed ? 2 : 1;
    2492                 :            : 
    2493                 :            :         /*
    2494                 :            :          * XXX For a bitmap index, since vacuum (or vacuum full) is currently done through
    2495                 :            :          * reindex_index, the reference count could be 2 (or 3). We set it
    2496                 :            :          * here until vacuum is done properly.
    2497                 :            :          */
    2498 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (expected_refcnt == 1 &&
    2499         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 RelationIsBitmapIndex(rel) &&
    2500         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 (rel->rd_refcnt == 2 || rel->rd_refcnt == 3))
    2501                 :          0 :                 expected_refcnt = rel->rd_refcnt;
    2502                 :            : 
    2503         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (rel->rd_refcnt != expected_refcnt)
    2504         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2505                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_IN_USE),
    2506                 :            :                                  /* translator: first %s is a SQL command, eg ALTER TABLE */
    2507                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot %s \"%s\" because "
    2508                 :            :                                                 "it is being used by active queries in this session",
    2509                 :            :                                                 stmt, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
    2510                 :            : 
    2511 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_INDEX &&
    2512                 :          0 :                 AfterTriggerPendingOnRel(RelationGetRelid(rel)))
    2513         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2514                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_IN_USE),
    2515                 :            :                                  /* translator: first %s is a SQL command, eg ALTER TABLE */
    2516                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot %s \"%s\" because "
    2517                 :            :                                                 "it has pending trigger events",
    2518                 :            :                                                 stmt, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
    2519                 :          0 : }
