   10207                 :            : /*
   10208                 :            :  * Copy data, block by block
   10209                 :            :  */
   10210                 :            : static void
   10211                 :            : copy_buffer_pool_data(Relation rel, SMgrRelation dst,
   10212                 :            :                                           ItemPointer persistentTid, int64 persistentSerialNum,
   10213                 :            :                                           bool useWal)
   10214                 :            : {
   10215                 :            :         SMgrRelation src;
   10216                 :            :         char       *buf;
   10217                 :            :         Page            page;
   10218                 :            :         BlockNumber nblocks;
   10219                 :            :         BlockNumber blkno;
   10220                 :            : 
   10221                 :            :         MirroredBufferPoolBulkLoadInfo bulkLoadInfo;
   10222                 :            : 
   10223                 :            :         /*
   10224                 :            :          * Since we copy the file directly without looking at the shared buffers,
   10225                 :            :          * we'd better first flush out any pages of the source relation that are
   10226                 :            :          * in shared buffers.  We assume no new changes will be made while we are
   10227                 :            :          * holding exclusive lock on the rel.
   10228                 :            :          */
   10229                 :          0 :         FlushRelationBuffers(rel);
   10230                 :            : 
   10231                 :            :         /*
   10232                 :            :          * palloc the buffer so that it's MAXALIGN'd.  If it were just a local
   10233                 :            :          * char[] array, the compiler might align it on any byte boundary, which
   10234                 :            :          * can seriously hurt transfer speed to and from the kernel; not to
   10235                 :            :          * mention possibly making log_newpage's accesses to the page header fail.
   10236                 :            :          */
   10237                 :          0 :         buf = (char *) palloc(BLCKSZ);
   10238                 :          0 :         page = (Page) buf;
   10239                 :            : 
   10240                 :          0 :         nblocks = RelationGetNumberOfBlocks(rel);
   10241                 :            : 
   10242                 :            :         /* RelationGetNumberOfBlocks will certainly have opened rd_smgr */
   10243                 :          0 :         src = rel->rd_smgr;
   10244                 :            : 
   10245         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!useWal)
   10246                 :            :         {
   10247                 :          0 :                 MirroredBufferPool_BeginBulkLoad(
   10248                 :          0 :                                                                 &rel->rd_node,
   10249                 :          0 :                                                                 persistentTid,
   10250                 :          0 :                                                                 persistentSerialNum,
   10251                 :            :                                                                 &bulkLoadInfo);
   10252                 :          0 :         }
   10253                 :            :         else
   10254                 :            :         {
   10255         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (Debug_persistent_print)
   10256                 :            :                 {
   10257                 :          0 :                         elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
   10258                 :            :                                  "copy_buffer_pool_data %u/%u/%u: not bypassing the WAL -- not using bulk load, persistent serial num " INT64_FORMAT ", TID %s",
   10259                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.spcNode,
   10260                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.dbNode,
   10261                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.relNode,
   10262                 :            :                                  persistentSerialNum,
   10263                 :            :                                  ItemPointerToString(persistentTid));
   10264                 :          0 :                 }
   10265 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 MemSet(&bulkLoadInfo, 0, sizeof(MirroredBufferPoolBulkLoadInfo));
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
                 [ #  # ]
   10266                 :            :         }
   10267                 :            :         
   10268         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (blkno = 0; blkno < nblocks; blkno++)
   10269                 :            :         {
   10270                 :            :                 /* If we got a cancel signal during the copy of the data, quit */
   10271 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
   10272                 :            : 
   10273                 :          0 :                 smgrread(src, blkno, buf);
   10274                 :            : 
   10275                 :          0 :                 PageSetChecksumInplace(page, blkno);
   10276                 :            : 
   10277                 :            :                 /* XLOG stuff */
   10278         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (useWal)
   10279                 :            :                 {
   10280                 :          0 :                         log_newpage_relFileNode(&dst->smgr_rnode, blkno, page, persistentTid,
   10281                 :          0 :                                                 persistentSerialNum);
   10282                 :          0 :                 }
   10283                 :            : 
   10284                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
   10285                 :          0 :                 LWLockAcquire(MirroredLock, LW_SHARED);
   10286                 :            :                 
   10287                 :            :                 /*
   10288                 :            :                  * Now write the page.  We say isTemp = true even if it's not a temp
   10289                 :            :                  * rel, because there's no need for smgr to schedule an fsync for this
   10290                 :            :                  * write; we'll do it ourselves below.
   10291                 :            :                  */
   10292                 :          0 :                 smgrextend(dst, blkno, buf, true);
   10293                 :            :                 
   10294                 :          0 :                 LWLockRelease(MirroredLock);
   10295                 :            :                 // -------- MirroredLock ----------
   10296                 :          0 :         }
   10297                 :            : 
   10298                 :          0 :         pfree(buf);
   10299                 :            : 
   10300                 :            :         /*
   10301                 :            :          * If the rel isn't temp, we must fsync it down to disk before it's safe
   10302                 :            :          * to commit the transaction.  (For a temp rel we don't care since the rel
   10303                 :            :          * will be uninteresting after a crash anyway.)
   10304                 :            :          *
   10305                 :            :          * It's obvious that we must do this when not WAL-logging the copy. It's
   10306                 :            :          * less obvious that we have to do it even if we did WAL-log the copied
   10307                 :            :          * pages. The reason is that since we're copying outside shared buffers, a
   10308                 :            :          * CHECKPOINT occurring during the copy has no way to flush the previously
   10309                 :            :          * written data to disk (indeed it won't know the new rel even exists).  A
   10310                 :            :          * crash later on would replay WAL from the checkpoint, therefore it
   10311                 :            :          * wouldn't replay our earlier WAL entries. If we do not fsync those pages
   10312                 :            :          * here, they might still not be on disk when the crash occurs.
   10313                 :            :          */
   10314                 :            :         
   10315                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
   10316                 :          0 :         LWLockAcquire(MirroredLock, LW_SHARED);
   10317                 :            :         
   10318         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!rel->rd_istemp)
   10319                 :          0 :                 smgrimmedsync(dst);
   10320                 :            :         
   10321                 :          0 :         LWLockRelease(MirroredLock);
   10322                 :            :         // -------- MirroredLock ----------
   10323                 :            : 
   10324         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!useWal)
   10325                 :            :         {
   10326                 :            :                 bool mirrorDataLossOccurred;
   10327                 :            :         
   10328                 :            :                 /*
   10329                 :            :                  * We may have to catch-up the mirror since bulk loading of data is
   10330                 :            :                  * ignored by resynchronize.
   10331                 :            :                  */
   10332                 :          0 :                 while (true)
   10333                 :            :                 {
   10334                 :            :                         bool bulkLoadFinished;
   10335                 :            :         
   10336                 :          0 :                         bulkLoadFinished = 
   10337                 :          0 :                                 MirroredBufferPool_EvaluateBulkLoadFinish(
   10338                 :            :                                                                                                 &bulkLoadInfo);
   10339                 :            :         
   10340         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (bulkLoadFinished)
   10341                 :            :                         {
   10342                 :            :                                 /*

   10345                 :            :                                  *
   10346                 :            :                                  * We have done a state-change from 'Bulk Load Create Pending'
   10347                 :            :                                  * to 'Create Pending'.
   10348                 :            :                                  */
   10349                 :          0 :                                 break;
   10350                 :            :                         }
   10351                 :            :         
   10352                 :            :                         /*
   10353                 :            :                          * Copy primary data to mirror and flush.
   10354                 :            :                          */
   10355                 :          0 :                         MirroredBufferPool_CopyToMirror(
   10356                 :          0 :                                                                         &rel->rd_node,
   10357                 :          0 :                                                                         rel->rd_rel->relname.data,
   10358                 :          0 :                                                                         persistentTid,
   10359                 :          0 :                                                                         persistentSerialNum,
   10360                 :          0 :                                                                         bulkLoadInfo.mirrorDataLossTrackingState,
   10361                 :          0 :                                                                         bulkLoadInfo.mirrorDataLossTrackingSessionNum,
   10362                 :          0 :                                                                         nblocks,
   10363                 :            :                                                                         &mirrorDataLossOccurred);
   10364                 :            :                 }
   10365                 :          0 :         }
   10366                 :            :         else
   10367                 :            :         {
   10368         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (Debug_persistent_print)
   10369                 :            :                 {
   10370                 :          0 :                         elog(Persistent_DebugPrintLevel(),
   10371                 :            :                                  "copy_buffer_pool_data %u/%u/%u: did not bypass the WAL -- did not use bulk load, persistent serial num " INT64_FORMAT ", TID %s",
   10372                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.spcNode,
   10373                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.dbNode,
   10374                 :            :                                  rel->rd_node.relNode,
   10375                 :            :                                  persistentSerialNum,
   10376                 :            :                                  ItemPointerToString(persistentTid));
   10377                 :          0 :                 }

   10434                 :            : static void
   10435                 :            : inherit_parent(Relation parent_rel, Relation child_rel, bool is_partition, List *inhAttrNameList)
   10436                 :            : {
   10437                 :            :         Relation        catalogRelation;
   10438                 :            :         SysScanDesc scan;
   10439                 :            :         ScanKeyData key;
   10440                 :            :         HeapTuple       inheritsTuple;
   10441                 :            :         int32           inhseqno;
   10442                 :            :         List       *children;
   10443                 :            : 
   10444                 :            :         /*
   10445                 :            :          * Check for duplicates in the list of parents, and determine the highest
   10446                 :            :          * inhseqno already present; we'll use the next one for the new parent.
   10447                 :            :          * (Note: get RowExclusiveLock because we will write pg_inherits below.)
   10448                 :            :          *
   10449                 :            :          * Note: we do not reject the case where the child already inherits from
   10450                 :            :          * the parent indirectly; CREATE TABLE doesn't reject comparable cases.
   10451                 :            :          */
   10452                 :          0 :         catalogRelation = heap_open(InheritsRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
   10453                 :          0 :         ScanKeyInit(&key,
   10454                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_inherits_inhrelid,
   10455                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
   10456                 :          0 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(child_rel)));
   10457                 :          0 :         scan = systable_beginscan(catalogRelation, InheritsRelidSeqnoIndexId,
   10458                 :            :                                                           true, SnapshotNow, 1, &key);
   10459                 :            : 
   10460                 :            :         /* inhseqno sequences start at 1 */
   10461                 :          0 :         inhseqno = 0;
   10462         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(inheritsTuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
   10463                 :            :         {
   10464                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_inherits inh = (Form_pg_inherits) GETSTRUCT(inheritsTuple);
   10465                 :            : 
   10466         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (inh->inhparent == RelationGetRelid(parent_rel))
   10467         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
   10468                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_TABLE),
   10469                 :            :                          errmsg("relation \"%s\" would be inherited from more than once",
   10470                 :            :                                         RelationGetRelationName(parent_rel))));
   10471         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (inh->inhseqno > inhseqno)
   10472                 :          0 :                         inhseqno = inh->inhseqno;
   10473                 :            :         }
   10474                 :          0 :         systable_endscan(scan);
   10475                 :            : 
   10476                 :            :         /*
   10477                 :            :          * Prevent circularity by seeing if proposed parent inherits from child.
   10478                 :            :          * (In particular, this disallows making a rel inherit from itself.)
   10479                 :            :          *
   10480                 :            :          * This is not completely bulletproof because of race conditions: in
   10481                 :            :          * multi-level inheritance trees, someone else could concurrently be
   10482                 :            :          * making another inheritance link that closes the loop but does not join
   10483                 :            :          * either of the rels we have locked.  Preventing that seems to require
   10484                 :            :          * exclusive locks on the entire inheritance tree, which is a cure worse
   10485                 :            :          * than the disease.  find_all_inheritors() will cope with circularity
   10486                 :            :          * anyway, so don't sweat it too much.
   10487                 :            :          */
   10488                 :          0 :         children = find_all_inheritors(RelationGetRelid(child_rel));
   10489                 :            : 
   10490         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (list_member_oid(children, RelationGetRelid(parent_rel)))
   10491         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   10492                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_TABLE),
   10493                 :            :                                  errmsg("circular inheritance not allowed"),
   10494                 :            :                                  errdetail("\"%s\" is already a child of \"%s\".",
   10495                 :            :                                                    RelationGetRelationName(parent_rel),
   10496                 :            :                                                    RelationGetRelationName(child_rel))));
   10497                 :            : 
   10498                 :            :         /* If parent has OIDs then child must have OIDs */
   10499 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (parent_rel->rd_rel->relhasoids && !child_rel->rd_rel->relhasoids)
   10500         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   10501                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
   10502                 :            :                                  errmsg("table \"%s\" without OIDs cannot inherit from table \"%s\" with OIDs",
   10503                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(child_rel),
   10504                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(parent_rel))));
   10505                 :            : 
   10506                 :            :         /* Match up the columns and bump attinhcount and attislocal */
   10507                 :          0 :         MergeAttributesIntoExisting(child_rel, parent_rel, inhAttrNameList, is_partition);
   10508                 :            : 
   10509                 :            :         /* Match up the constraints and make sure they're present in child */
   10510                 :          0 :         MergeConstraintsIntoExisting(child_rel, parent_rel);
   10511                 :            : 
   10512                 :            :         /*
   10513                 :            :          * OK, it looks valid.  Make the catalog entries that show inheritance.
   10514                 :            :          */
   10515                 :          0 :         StoreCatalogInheritance1(RelationGetRelid(child_rel),
   10516                 :          0 :                                                          RelationGetRelid(parent_rel),
   10517                 :          0 :                                                          inhseqno + 1,
   10518                 :          0 :                                                          catalogRelation, is_partition);
   10519                 :            : 
   10520                 :            :         /* Now we're done with pg_inherits */
   10521                 :          0 :         heap_close(catalogRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
   10522                 :            : 
   10523                 :          0 : }
   10524                 :            : 
   10525                 :            : /*
   10526                 :            :  * ALTER TABLE INHERIT
   10527                 :            :  *
   10528                 :            :  * Add a parent to the child's parents. This verifies that all the columns and
   10529                 :            :  * check constraints of the parent appear in the child and that they have the
   10530                 :            :  * same data types and expressions.
   10531                 :            :  */
   10532                 :            : static void
   10533                 :            : ATExecAddInherit(Relation child_rel, Node *node)
   10534                 :            : {
   10535                 :            :         Relation        parent_rel;
   10536                 :            :         bool            is_partition;
   10537                 :            :         RangeVar   *parent;
   10538                 :          0 :         List       *inhAttrNameList = NIL;
   10539                 :            : 
   10540 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(PointerIsValid(node));
   10541                 :            : 
   10542         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (IsA(node, InheritPartitionCmd))
   10543                 :            :         {
   10544                 :          0 :                 parent = ((InheritPartitionCmd *) node)->parent;
   10545                 :          0 :                 is_partition = true;
   10546                 :          0 :         }
   10547                 :            :         else
   10548                 :            :         {
   10549                 :          0 :                 parent = (RangeVar *) node;
   10550                 :          0 :                 is_partition = false;
   10551                 :            :         }
   10552                 :            : 
   10553                 :            :         /*
   10554                 :            :          * AccessShareLock on the parent is what's obtained during normal CREATE
   10555                 :            :          * TABLE ... INHERITS ..., so should be enough here.
   10556                 :            :          */
   10557                 :          0 :         parent_rel = heap_openrv(parent, AccessShareLock);
   10558                 :            : 
   10559                 :            :         /*
   10560                 :            :          * Must be owner of both parent and child -- child was checked by
   10561                 :            :          * ATSimplePermissions call in ATPrepCmd
   10562                 :            :          */
   10563                 :          0 :         ATSimplePermissions(parent_rel, false);
   10564                 :            : 
   10565                 :            :         /* Permanent rels cannot inherit from temporary ones */
   10566 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!isTempNamespace(RelationGetNamespace(child_rel)) &&
   10567                 :          0 :                 isTempNamespace(RelationGetNamespace(parent_rel)))
   10568         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   10569                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
   10570                 :            :                                  errmsg("cannot inherit from temporary relation \"%s\"",
   10571                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(parent_rel))));
   10572                 :            : 
   10573         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (is_partition)
   10574                 :            :         {
   10575                 :            :                 /* lookup all attrs */
   10576                 :            :                 int attno;
   10577                 :            : 
   10578         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 for (attno = 0; attno < parent_rel->rd_att->natts; attno++)
   10579                 :            :                 {
   10580                 :          0 :                         Form_pg_attribute        attribute = parent_rel->rd_att->attrs[attno];
   10581                 :          0 :                         char                            *attributeName = NameStr(attribute->attname);
   10582                 :            : 
   10583                 :            :                         /* MPP-5397: ignore dropped cols */
   10584         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!attribute->attisdropped)
   10585                 :          0 :                                 inhAttrNameList =
   10586                 :          0 :                                                 lappend(inhAttrNameList,
   10587                 :          0 :                                                                 makeString(attributeName));
   10588                 :          0 :                 }
   10589                 :            : 
   10590                 :          0 :         }
   10591                 :          0 :         inherit_parent(parent_rel, child_rel, is_partition, inhAttrNameList);
   10592                 :            : 
   10593                 :            :         /*
   10594                 :            :          * Keep our lock on the parent relation until commit, unless we're
   10595                 :            :          * doing partitioning, in which case the parent is sufficiently locked.
   10596                 :            :          * We want to unlock here in case we're doing deep sub partitioning. We do
   10597                 :            :          * not want to acquire too many locks since we're overflow the lock buffer.
   10598                 :            :          * An exclusive lock on the parent table is sufficient to guard against
   10599                 :            :          * concurrency issues.
   10600                 :            :          */
   10601         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (is_partition)
   10602                 :          0 :                 heap_close(parent_rel, AccessShareLock);
   10603                 :            :         else
   10604                 :          0 :                 heap_close(parent_rel, NoLock);
   10605                 :            : 
   10606                 :            :         /* MPP-6929: metadata tracking */
   10607         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if ((Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)
   10608         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 && MetaTrackValidKindNsp(child_rel->rd_rel))
   10609                 :          0 :                 MetaTrackUpdObject(RelationRelationId,
   10610                 :          0 :                                                    RelationGetRelid(child_rel),
   10611                 :          0 :                                                    GetUserId(),
   10612                 :            :                                                    "ALTER", "INHERIT"
   10613                 :            :                                 );
   10614                 :          0 : }
   10615                 :            : 
   10616                 :            : /*
   10617                 :            :  * Obtain the source-text form of the constraint expression for a check
   10618                 :            :  * constraint, given its pg_constraint tuple
   10619                 :            :  */
   10620                 :            : static char *
   10621                 :            : decompile_conbin(HeapTuple contup, TupleDesc tupdesc)
   10622                 :            : {
   10623                 :            :         Form_pg_constraint con;
   10624                 :            :         bool            isnull;
   10625                 :            :         Datum           attr;
   10626                 :            :         Datum           expr;
   10627                 :            : 
   10628                 :          0 :         con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(contup);
   10629                 :          0 :         attr = heap_getattr(contup, Anum_pg_constraint_conbin, tupdesc, &isnull);

   10638                 :            : /*
   10639                 :            :  * Check columns in child table match up with columns in parent, and increment
   10640                 :            :  * their attinhcount.
   10641                 :            :  *
   10642                 :            :  * Called by ATExecAddInherit
   10643                 :            :  *
   10644                 :            :  * Currently all parent columns must be found in child. Missing columns are an
   10645                 :            :  * error.  One day we might consider creating new columns like CREATE TABLE
   10646                 :            :  * does.  However, that is widely unpopular --- in the common use case of
   10647                 :            :  * partitioned tables it's a foot-gun.
   10648                 :            :  *
   10649                 :            :  * The data type must match exactly. If the parent column is NOT NULL then
   10650                 :            :  * the child must be as well. Defaults are not compared, however.
   10651                 :            :  */
   10652                 :            : static void
   10653                 :            : MergeAttributesIntoExisting(Relation child_rel, Relation parent_rel, List *inhAttrNameList,
   10654                 :            :                                                         bool is_partition)

   10656                 :            :         Relation        attrrel;

   10668                 :          0 :         constr = tupleDesc->constr;
   10669                 :            : 
   10670                 :            :         /*
   10671                 :            :          * If we have an inherited column list, ensure all named columns exist in parent
   10672                 :            :          * and that the list excludes system columns.
   10673                 :            :          */

   10679 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 for (parent_attno = 1; parent_attno <= parent_natts && !columnDefined; parent_attno++)
   10680                 :            :                 {

   10682                 :          0 :                         Form_pg_attribute attribute = tupleDesc->attrs[parent_attno - 1];
   10683         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (attribute->attisdropped)
   10684                 :          0 :                                 continue;
   10685                 :          0 :                         attributeName = NameStr(attribute->attname);

   10687                 :          0 :                 }
   10688                 :            : 
   10689         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!columnDefined)

   10693                 :            :                                                         inhAttrName,

   10695                 :          0 :         }
   10696                 :            :

   10700                 :          0 :                 char       *attributeName = NameStr(attribute->attname);
   10701                 :            : 
   10702                 :            :                 /* Ignore dropped columns in the parent. */

   10704                 :          0 :                         continue;
   10705                 :            :

   10708                 :          0 :                                                                                   attributeName);

   10713                 :            : 
   10714 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (attribute->atttypid != childatt->atttypid ||
   10715                 :          0 :                                 attribute->atttypmod != childatt->atttypmod)
   10716         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
   10717                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
   10718                 :            :                                                  errmsg("child table \"%s\" has different type for column \"%s\"",
   10719                 :            :                                                                 RelationGetRelationName(child_rel),
   10720                 :            :                                                                 attributeName)));
   10721                 :            : 
   10722 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (attribute->attnotnull && !childatt->attnotnull)
   10723         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
   10724                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),

   10751                 :            :                                                 {
   10752                 :          0 :                                                         childatt->attislocal = false;
   10753                 :          0 :                                                         break;
   10754                 :            :                                                 }
   10755                 :          0 :                                         }
   10756                 :            :                                 }
   10757                 :          0 :                         }
   10758                 :            : 
   10759                 :          0 :                         simple_heap_update(attrrel, &tuple->t_self, tuple);

   10762                 :          0 :                 }

   10764                 :            :                 {
   10765         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
   10766                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),

   10769                 :            :                 }
   10770                 :          0 :         }
   10771                 :            : 
   10772                 :          0 :         heap_close(attrrel, RowExclusiveLock);
   10773                 :          0 : }
   10774                 :            : 
   10775                 :            : /*
   10776                 :            :  * Check constraints in child table match up with constraints in parent
   10777                 :            :  *
   10778                 :            :  * Called by ATExecAddInherit and exchange_part_inheritance
   10779                 :            :  *
   10780                 :            :  * Currently all constraints in parent must be present in the child. One day we
   10781                 :            :  * may consider adding new constraints like CREATE TABLE does. We may also want
   10782                 :            :  * to allow an optional flag on parent table constraints indicating they are
   10783                 :            :  * intended to ONLY apply to the master table, not to the children. That would
   10784                 :            :  * make it possible to ensure no records are mistakenly inserted into the
   10785                 :            :  * master in partitioned tables rather than the appropriate child.
   10786                 :            :  *
   10787                 :            :  * XXX This is O(N^2) which may be an issue with tables with hundreds of
   10788                 :            :  * constraints. As long as tables have more like 10 constraints it shouldn't be
   10789                 :            :  * a problem though. Even 100 constraints ought not be the end of the world.
   10790                 :            :  */
   10791                 :            : static void
   10792                 :            : MergeConstraintsIntoExisting(Relation child_rel, Relation parent_rel)
   10793                 :            : {
   10794                 :            :         Relation        catalogRelation;

   10801                 :            : 
   10802                 :            :         /* First gather up the child's constraint definitions */
   10803                 :          0 :         catalogRelation = heap_open(ConstraintRelationId, AccessShareLock);
   10804                 :          0 :         tupleDesc = RelationGetDescr(catalogRelation);
   10805                 :            : 
   10806                 :          0 :         ScanKeyInit(&key,
   10807                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
   10808                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
   10809                 :          0 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(child_rel)));
   10810                 :          0 :         scan = systable_beginscan(catalogRelation, ConstraintRelidIndexId,
   10811                 :            :                                                           true, SnapshotNow, 1, &key);
   10812                 :            : 
   10813                 :          0 :         constraints = NIL;
   10814         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(constraintTuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
   10815                 :            :         {
   10816                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(constraintTuple);
   10817                 :            : 
   10818         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (con->contype != CONSTRAINT_CHECK)
   10819                 :          0 :                         continue;
   10820                 :            : 
   10821                 :          0 :                 constraints = lappend(constraints, heap_copytuple(constraintTuple));
   10822                 :            :         }
   10823                 :            : 
   10824                 :          0 :         systable_endscan(scan);
   10825                 :            : 
   10826                 :            :         /* Then scan through the parent's constraints looking for matches */
   10827                 :          0 :         ScanKeyInit(&key,
   10828                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
   10829                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
   10830                 :          0 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(parent_rel)));
   10831                 :          0 :         scan = systable_beginscan(catalogRelation, ConstraintRelidIndexId, true,
   10832                 :            :                                                           SnapshotNow, 1, &key);
   10833                 :            : 
   10834         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(constraintTuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
   10835                 :            :         {
   10836                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_constraint parent_con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(constraintTuple);
   10837                 :          0 :                 bool            found = false;
   10838                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_constraint child_con = NULL;
   10839                 :          0 :                 HeapTuple       child_contuple = NULL;
   10840                 :            : 
   10841         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (parent_con->contype != CONSTRAINT_CHECK)
   10842                 :          0 :                         continue;
   10843                 :            : 
   10844         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 foreach(elem, constraints)
   10845                 :            :                 {
   10846                 :          0 :                         child_contuple = (HeapTuple) lfirst(elem);

   10849                 :          0 :                                            NameStr(child_con->conname)) == 0)

   10852                 :          0 :                                 break;

   10854                 :          0 :                 }

   10856         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!found)
   10857         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,

   10860                 :            :                                                         NameStr(parent_con->conname))));

   10881                 :            : /*
   10882                 :            :  * ALTER TABLE NO INHERIT
   10883                 :            :  *
   10884                 :            :  * Drop a parent from the child's parents. This just adjusts the attinhcount
   10885                 :            :  * and attislocal of the columns and removes the pg_inherit and pg_depend
   10886                 :            :  * entries.
   10887                 :            :  *
   10888                 :            :  * If attinhcount goes to 0 then attislocal gets set to true. If it goes back
   10889                 :            :  * up attislocal stays true, which means if a child is ever removed from a
   10890                 :            :  * parent then its columns will never be automatically dropped which may
   10891                 :            :  * surprise. But at least we'll never surprise by dropping columns someone
   10892                 :            :  * isn't expecting to be dropped which would actually mean data loss.
   10893                 :            :  */

   10895                 :            : ATExecDropInherit(Relation rel, RangeVar *parent, bool is_partition)
   10896                 :            : {

   10904                 :          0 :         bool            found = false;

   10912                 :            :

   10915                 :            :          * the child is presumed enough rights.

   10917                 :            :

   10939                 :          0 :                         break;
   10940                 :            :                 }
   10941                 :            :         }

   10945                 :            : 
   10946         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!found)
   10947         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
   10948                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),

   10951                 :            :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(rel))));

   10959                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
   10960                 :          0 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));

   10962                 :          0 :                                                           true, SnapshotNow, 1, key);
   10963         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(attributeTuple = systable_getnext(scan)))

   10971                 :          0 :                         continue;

   11018                 :            :

   11030                 :          0 :         heap_close(catalogRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
   11031                 :            : 
   11032                 :            :         /* keep our lock on the parent relation until commit */
   11033                 :          0 :         heap_close(parent_rel, NoLock);
   11034                 :            :

   11045                 :            : /*

   11072                 :            : /*
   11073                 :            :  * Update the pg_attribute entries of dropped columns in given relation,
   11074                 :            :  * as if they were of type int4.
   11075                 :            :  *
   11076                 :            :  * This is used by ALTER TABLE SET DISTRIBUTED BY, which swaps the
   11077                 :            :  * relation file with a newly constructed temp table. The temp table is
   11078                 :            :  * constructed with int4 columns standing in for the dropped columns,
   11079                 :            :  * and this function is used to update the original table's definition
   11080                 :            :  * to match that.
   11081                 :            :  */
   11082                 :            : static void
   11083                 :            : change_dropped_col_datatypes(Relation rel)
   11084                 :            : {
   11085                 :            :         Relation        catalogRelation;
   11086                 :            :         SysScanDesc scan;
   11087                 :            :         ScanKeyData key;
   11088                 :            :         HeapTuple       tuple;
   11089                 :            : 
   11090                 :            :         /*
   11091                 :            :          * Loop through all dropped columns.
   11092                 :            :          */
   11093                 :          0 :         catalogRelation = heap_open(AttributeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
   11094                 :          0 :         ScanKeyInit(&key,
   11095                 :            :                                 Anum_pg_attribute_attrelid,
   11096                 :            :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
   11097                 :          0 :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));
   11098                 :          0 :         scan = systable_beginscan(catalogRelation, AttributeRelidNumIndexId,
   11099                 :            :                                                           true, SnapshotNow, 1, &key);
   11100         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
   11101                 :            :         {
   11102                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_attribute att = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
   11103                 :            :                 HeapTuple copyTuple;
   11104                 :            : 
   11105         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (att->attisdropped)
   11106                 :            :                 {
   11107 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(att->attnum > 0 && att->attnum <= RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel));
                 [ #  # ]
   11108                 :            : 
   11109                 :          0 :                         copyTuple = heap_copytuple(tuple);
   11110                 :          0 :                         att = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(copyTuple);
   11111                 :            : 
   11112                 :          0 :                         att->attlen = sizeof(int32);
   11113                 :          0 :                         att->attndims = 0;
   11114                 :          0 :                         att->atttypmod = -1;
   11115                 :          0 :                         att->attbyval = true;
   11116                 :          0 :                         att->attstorage = 'p';
   11117                 :          0 :                         att->attalign = 'i';
   11118                 :            : 
   11119                 :          0 :                         simple_heap_update(catalogRelation, &tuple->t_self, copyTuple);
   11120                 :          0 :                         CatalogUpdateIndexes(catalogRelation, copyTuple);
   11121                 :          0 :                 }
   11122                 :            :         }
   11123                 :          0 :         systable_endscan(scan);
   11124                 :            : 
   11125                 :          0 :         heap_close(catalogRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
   11126                 :          0 : }

   11128                 :            : /*
   11129                 :            :  * Build:
   11130                 :            :  *

   11164                 :            :

   11180                 :          0 :         }
   11181                 :            : 
   11182                 :          0 :         from_tbl = makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(rel)),
   11183                 :          0 :                                                         pstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(rel)), -1);

   11185                 :            :                                                                 * Otherwise, the data from the child

   11187                 :            :                                                                 */

   11202                 :            : 
   11203                 :          0 :                 i->relation = tmprel;

   11206                 :          0 :         }

   11210                 :            :

   11217                 :            :         }

   11219                 :            : 
   11220                 :          0 :         q = parse_analyze((Node *)n, NULL, NULL, 0);

   11305                 :            : static RangeVar *
   11306                 :            : make_temp_table_name(Relation rel, BackendId id)

   11317                 :            : 
   11318                 :            : /*
   11319                 :            :  * If the table has dropped columns, we must create the table and
   11320                 :            :  * drop the columns before we can dispatch the select statement.
   11321                 :            :  * Return true if we do it, false if we do not. If we return false,
   11322                 :            :  * there are no dropped columns and we can do a SELECT INTO later.
   11323                 :            :  * If we need to do it, but fail, issue an error. (See make_type.)
   11324                 :            :  *
   11325                 :            :  * Specifically for build_ctas_with_dist.
   11326                 :            :  *

   11328                 :            :  * a value that matches 'opts'.
   11329                 :            :  */
   11330                 :            : static bool
   11331                 :            : prebuild_temp_table(Relation rel, RangeVar *tmpname, List *distro, List *opts,
   11332                 :            :                                         bool isTmpTableAo, bool useExistingColumnAttributes)
   11333                 :            : {
   11334                 :          0 :         bool need_rebuild = false;

   11337                 :            : 
   11338                 :            :         /*

   11344                 :            : 
   11345         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!need_rebuild)
   11346                 :            :         {

   11348                 :            :                 {
   11349         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (tupdesc->attrs[attno]->attisdropped)
   11350                 :            :                         {
   11351                 :          0 :                                 need_rebuild = true;
   11352                 :          0 :                                 break;
   11353                 :            :                         }
   11354                 :          0 :                 }
   11355                 :          0 :         }
   11356                 :            : 
   11357                 :            :         /*
   11358                 :            :          * If the new table is an AO table with indexes, always use

   11370                 :            :         {

   11375                 :            :

   11382                 :            :

   11384                 :          0 :                         rel->rd_rel->relhasindex)
   11385                 :          0 :                         cs->buildAoBlkdir = true;
   11386                 :            : 
   11387         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (RelationIsAoCols(rel))

   11414                 :            :

   11416                 :            :                                 {

   11419                 :          0 :                                 }

   11421                 :          0 :                 }

   11423                 :          0 :                         cs->options = opts;
   11424                 :            :

   11434                 :            :                         {

   11445                 :          0 :                                 tname = makeTypeNameFromOid(INT4OID, -1);

   11447                 :          0 :                         }
   11448                 :            :                         else
   11449                 :            :                         {
   11450                 :          0 :                                 Type typ = typeidType(att->atttypid);
   11451                 :          0 :                                 Oid typnamespace = ((Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(typ))->typnamespace;
   11452                 :          0 :                                 char *nspname = get_namespace_name(typnamespace);
   11453                 :            :                                 int arno;
   11454                 :            :                                 char *typstr;
   11455                 :          0 :                                 int4 ndims = att->attndims;
   11456                 :            : 
   11457 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 tname = makeNode(TypeName);
   11458                 :            : 
   11459         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (!PointerIsValid(nspname))
   11460                 :          0 :                                         elog(ERROR, "could not lookup namespace %d", typnamespace);
   11461                 :          0 :                                 cd->colname = pstrdup(NameStr(att->attname));
   11462                 :          0 :                                 typstr = typeTypeName(typ);
   11463                 :          0 :                                 tname->names = list_make2(makeString(nspname),
   11464                 :            :                                                                                   makeString(typstr));
   11465                 :          0 :                                 ReleaseSysCache(typ);
   11466                 :          0 :                                 tname->typemod = att->atttypmod;
   11467                 :            : 
   11468                 :            :                                 /*
   11469                 :            :                                  * If this is a built in array type, like _int4, then reduce
   11470                 :            :                                  * the array dimensions by 1. This is an annoying postgres
   11471                 :            :                                  * hack which I wish would go away.
   11472                 :            :                                  */

   11476         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 for (arno = 0; arno < ndims; arno++)

   11484                 :            :                         }
   11485                 :            : 
   11486                 :          0 :                         tname->location = -1;
   11487                 :          0 :                         cd->typeName = tname;
   11488                 :          0 :                         cs->tableElts = lappend(cs->tableElts, cd);
   11489                 :          0 :                 }
   11490                 :          0 :                 q = parse_analyze((Node *)cs, NULL, NULL, 0);
   11491                 :          0 :                 ProcessUtility((Node *)q->utilityStmt,
   11492                 :          0 :                                            synthetic_sql,
   11493                 :            :                                            NULL,
   11494                 :            :                                            false, /* not top level */
   11495                 :          0 :                                            dest,
   11496                 :            :                                            NULL);
   11497                 :          0 :                 CommandCounterIncrement();
   11498                 :          0 :         }
   11499                 :          0 :         return need_rebuild;
   11500                 :            : }

   11502                 :            : /* Build a human readable tag for what we're doing */

   11507                 :            : 
   11508 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (lwith && ldistro)
   11509                 :          0 :                 distro_str = "SET WITH DISTRIBUTED BY";

   11513                 :          0 :                         distro_str = "SET WITH";

   11515                 :          0 :                         distro_str = "SET DISTRIBUTED BY";
   11516                 :            :         }
   11517                 :          0 :         return pstrdup(distro_str); /* don't return a stack address */

   11520                 :            : /*
   11521                 :            :  * Check if distribution key compatible with unique index
   11522                 :            :  */
   11523                 :            : static void checkUniqueIndexCompatible(Relation rel, GpPolicy *pol)

   11525                 :          0 :         List *indexoidlist = RelationGetIndexList(rel);
   11526                 :          0 :         ListCell *indexoidscan = NULL;
   11527                 :          0 :         Bitmapset *polbm = NULL;
   11528                 :          0 :         int i = 0;
   11529                 :            : 
   11530 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if(pol == NULL || pol->nattrs == 0)
   11531                 :          0 :                 return;
   11532                 :            : 
   11533         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < pol->nattrs; i++)
   11534                 :          0 :                 polbm = bms_add_member(polbm, pol->attrs[i]);
   11535                 :            : 
   11536                 :            :         /* Loop over all indexes on the relation */
   11537         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(indexoidscan, indexoidlist)
   11538                 :            :         {
   11539                 :          0 :                 Oid                     indexoid = lfirst_oid(indexoidscan);

   11541                 :            :                 Form_pg_index indexStruct;
   11542                 :            :                 int                     i;
   11543                 :          0 :                 Bitmapset  *indbm = NULL;
   11544                 :            : 
   11545                 :          0 :                 indexTuple = SearchSysCache1(INDEXRELID,
   11546                 :            :                                                                          ObjectIdGetDatum(indexoid));
   11547         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(indexTuple))
   11548                 :          0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for index %u", indexoid);
   11549                 :          0 :                 indexStruct = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(indexTuple);
   11550                 :            : 
   11551                 :            :                 /* If the index is not a unique key, skip the check */
   11552         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (indexStruct->indisunique)
   11553                 :            :                 {
   11554         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         for (i = 0; i < indexStruct->indnatts; i++)
   11555                 :            :                         {
   11556                 :          0 :                                 indbm = bms_add_member(indbm, indexStruct->indkey.values[i]);
   11557                 :          0 :                         }
   11558                 :            : 
   11559         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!bms_is_subset(polbm, indbm))
   11560                 :            :                         {
   11561         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
   11562                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   11563                 :            :                                                  errmsg("UNIQUE INDEX and DISTRIBUTED BY definitions incompatible"),
   11564                 :            :                                                  errhint("the DISTRIBUTED BY columns must be equal to "
   11565                 :            :                                                                  "or a left-subset of the UNIQUE INDEX columns.")));
   11566                 :          0 :                         }
   11567                 :            : 
   11568                 :          0 :                         bms_free(indbm);
   11569                 :          0 :                 }
   11570                 :            : 
   11571                 :          0 :                 ReleaseSysCache(indexTuple);
   11572                 :          0 :         }
   11573                 :            : 
   11574                 :          0 :         list_free(indexoidlist);
   11575                 :          0 :         bms_free(polbm);
   11576                 :          0 : }

   11634                 :            :

   11656                 :            :                         {

   11658                 :            :

   11660                 :            :                                 {

   11665                 :            :                                                         errmsg("option \"%s\" not supported", def->defname)));
   11666                 :            : 
   11667         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (pg_strcasecmp(def->defname, "appendonly") == 0)
   11668                 :            :                                         {
   11669 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 if (IsA(def->arg, String) && pg_strcasecmp(strVal(def->arg), "true") == 0)
   11670                 :            :                                                 {
   11671                 :          0 :                                                         is_ao = true;
   11672                 :          0 :                                                         relstorage = RELSTORAGE_AOROWS;
   11673                 :          0 :                                                 }

   11675                 :          0 :                                                         is_ao = false;
   11676                 :          0 :                                         }

   11688                 :            :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   11689                 :            :                                                                         errmsg("invalid orientation option"),
   11690                 :            :                                                                         errhint("valid orientation option is \"column\" or \"row\"")));
   11691                 :            :                                                 }
   11692                 :          0 :                                         }
   11693                 :            :                                         else
   11694                 :            :                                         {
   11695                 :          0 :                                                 useExistingColumnAttributes=false;
   11696                 :            :                                         }
   11697                 :            : 
   11698                 :          0 :                                         nlist = lappend(nlist, def);
   11699                 :          0 :                                 }
   11700                 :            :                                 else
   11701                 :            :                                 {
   11702                 :            :                                         /* have we been here before ? */
   11703         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (seen_reorg)
   11704         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   11705                 :            :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   11706                 :            :                                                         errmsg("\"%s\" specified more than once", reorg_str)));
   11707                 :            : 
   11708                 :          0 :                                         seen_reorg = true;
   11709         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (!def->arg)
   11710                 :          0 :                                                 force_reorg = true;
   11711                 :            :                                         else
   11712                 :            :                                         {
   11713 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 if (IsA(def->arg, String) && pg_strcasecmp("TRUE", strVal(def->arg)) == 0)
   11714                 :          0 :                                                         force_reorg = true;

   11716                 :          0 :                                                         force_reorg = false;
   11717                 :            :                                                 else
   11718         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   11719                 :            :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   11720                 :            :                                                                 errmsg("Invalid REORGANIZE option"),
   11721                 :            :                                                                 errhint("valid REORGANIZE option is \"true\" or \"false\"")));
   11722                 :            :                                         }
   11723                 :            :                                 }
   11724                 :          0 :                         }

   11727                 :            :                         {

   11761                 :            :                 }

   11763         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (ldistro)

   11807                 :            : 
   11808                 :            :         /*
   11809                 :            :          * Changing a table from random distribution to a specific distribution
   11810                 :            :          * policy is the hard bit. For that, we must do the following:
   11811                 :            :          *
   11812                 :            :          * a) Ensure that the proposed policy is sensible
   11813                 :            :          * b) Create a temporary table and reorganise data according to
   11814                 :            :          * our desired distribution policy. To do this, we build a Query
   11815                 :            :          * node which expresses the query CREATE TABLE tmp_table_name AS
   11816                 :            :          * SELECT * FROM cur_table DISTRIBUTED BY (policy)
   11817                 :            :          * c) Execute the query across all nodes
   11818                 :            :          * d) Update our parse tree to include the details of the newly created
   11819                 :            :          * table
   11820                 :            :          * e) Update the ownership of the temporary table
   11821                 :            :          * f) Swap the relfilenodes of the existing table and the temporary table
   11822                 :            :          * g) Update the policy on the QD to reflect the underlying data
   11823                 :            :          * h) Drop the temporary table -- and with it, the old copy of the data
   11824                 :            :          */
   11825         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)
   11826                 :            :         {
   11827                 :          0 :                 volatile Snapshot saveSnapshot = NULL;
   11828         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (change_policy)
   11829                 :            :                 {
   11830                 :          0 :                         policy = palloc(sizeof(GpPolicy) + sizeof(policy->attrs[0]) * list_length(ldistro));
   11831                 :          0 :                         policy->ptype = POLICYTYPE_PARTITIONED;
   11832                 :          0 :                         policy->nattrs = 0;
   11833                 :            : 
   11834                 :            :                         /* Step (a) */
   11835         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!rand_pol)
   11836                 :            :                         {
   11837         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 foreach(lc, ldistro)
   11838                 :            :                                 {
   11839                 :          0 :                                         char       *colName = strVal((Value *)lfirst(lc));
   11840                 :            :                                         HeapTuple       tuple;
   11841                 :            :                                         AttrNumber      attnum;
   11842                 :            : 
   11843                 :          0 :                                         tuple = SearchSysCacheAttName(RelationGetRelid(rel), colName);
   11844                 :            : 
   11845         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
   11846         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   11847                 :            :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
   11848                 :            :                                                                  errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
   11849                 :            :                                                                                 colName,
   11850                 :            :                                                                                 RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
   11851                 :            : 
   11852                 :          0 :                                         attnum = ((Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(tuple))->attnum;
   11853                 :            : 
   11854                 :            :                                         /* Prevent them from altering a system attribute */
   11855         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (attnum <= 0)
   11856         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   11857                 :            :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
   11858                 :            :                                                                  errmsg("cannot distribute by system column \"%s\"",
   11859                 :            :                                                                                 colName)));
   11860                 :            : 
   11861                 :          0 :                                         policy->attrs[policy->nattrs++] = attnum;
   11862                 :            : 
   11863                 :          0 :                                         ReleaseSysCache(tuple);
   11864                 :          0 :                                         cols = lappend(cols, lfirst(lc));
   11865                 :          0 :                                 } /* end foreach */
   11866                 :            : 
   11867 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 Assert(policy->nattrs > 0);
   11868                 :            : 
   11869                 :            :                                 /*
   11870                 :            :                                  * See if the the old policy is the same as the new one but
   11871                 :            :                                  * remember, we still might have to rebuild if there are new
   11872                 :            :                                  * storage options.
   11873                 :            :                                  */
   11874 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (!DatumGetPointer(newOptions) && !force_reorg &&
                 [ #  # ]
   11875                 :          0 :                                         (policy->nattrs == rel->rd_cdbpolicy->nattrs))
   11876                 :            :                                 {
   11877                 :            :                                         int i;
   11878                 :          0 :                                         bool diff = false;
   11879                 :            : 
   11880         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         for (i = 0; i < policy->nattrs; i++)
   11881                 :            :                                         {
   11882         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 if (policy->attrs[i] != rel->rd_cdbpolicy->attrs[i])
   11883                 :            :                                                 {
   11884                 :          0 :                                                         diff = true;
   11885                 :          0 :                                                         break;
   11886                 :            :                                                 }
   11887                 :          0 :                                         }
   11888         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (!diff)
   11889                 :            :                                         {
   11890                 :            :                                                 /*
   11891                 :            :                                                  * This string length calculation relies on that we add
   11892                 :            :                                                  * a comma after each column entry except the last one,
   11893                 :            :                                                  * at which point the string should be NULL terminated
   11894                 :            :                                                  * instead.
   11895                 :            :                                                  */
   11896                 :          0 :                                                 char *dist = palloc(list_length(ldistro) * (NAMEDATALEN + 1));
   11897                 :            : 
   11898                 :          0 :                                                 dist[0] = '\0';

   11901                 :            :                                                 {

   11903                 :          0 :                                                                 strcat(dist, ",");

   11907                 :            :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),

   11915                 :            :                                                                 "to force redistribution",

   11917                 :            :                                                                 dist)));
   11918                 :          0 :                                                 heap_close(rel, NoLock);

   11925                 :            :                                         }
   11926                 :          0 :                                 }
   11927                 :          0 :                         }

   11929                 :          0 :                         checkUniqueIndexCompatible(rel, policy);

   11932         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!ldistro)

   11934                 :            : 
   11935                 :            :                 /*
   11936                 :            :                  * Force the use of legacy query optimizer, since PQO will not

   11939                 :            :                  */
